old_claim.note: Military Map Rooms provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains because military_map_rooms is scoped to the Depot de la Guerre map and archive office, while military_engineer_corps is now scoped to Vauban's 1690 corps de Genie. The inspected Depot source does not make the Depot a prerequisite for forming that engineer corps.

source_shape.source_locator: Ministere de la Culture glossary entry dates the Depot de la Guerre to 1688 and describes it as a bureau producing and conserving military maps.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source places the corrected Depot node as a distinct 1688 military map and archive bureau.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source describes the Depot as an archive/cartography bureau, not as the cause of the Vauban-era engineer corps. A direct edge would require a separate source showing institutional dependency.

ontology_before: Military Engineer Corps depended directly on the generic Military Map Rooms node through an unsourced expert-inference edge.
ontology_after: Early Modern Royal Engineer Corps no longer depends directly on the Depot de la Guerre node; its remaining direct prerequisites cover fortification schooling and technical drawing.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: direct military_engineer_corps -> military_map_rooms edge removed.
would_reject_if: A source showed Vauban's corps de Genie formation depended on the Depot de la Guerre.
The military_map_rooms node were split and an earlier source-backed military-map archive became available.
The military_engineer_corps node were narrowed to a Depot-specific cartography office rather than a royal engineer corps.
short_reason: The corrected Depot node is a map/archive bureau, not a documented prerequisite for the royal engineer corps.
